heading: Commission to serve against friendly nation
label: 958 Commission to serve against friendly nation
text: Any citizen of the United States who, within the jurisdiction thereof, accepts and exercises a commission to serve a foreign prince, state, colony, district, or people, in war, against any prince, state, colony, district, or people, with whom the United States is at peace, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than three years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 21 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 935 Stat. 1089Mandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.Minor changes in phraseology were made.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $2,000”.Pub. L. 103–322
